description.en: An entry in a SymbolTable corresponding to an identifier in the source. The constructor is not public. get_name()¶ Return the symbol’s name. is_referenced()¶ Return True if the symbol is used in its block. is_imported()¶ Return True if the symbol is created from an import statement. is_parameter()¶ Return True if the symbol is a parameter. is_type_parameter()¶ Return True if the symbol is a type parameter. Added in version 3.14. is_global()¶ Return True if the symbol is global. is_nonlocal()¶...

description.en: A namespace table for a block. The constructor is not public. get_type()¶ Return the type of the symbol table. Possible values are members of the SymbolTableType enumeration. Changed in version 3.12: Added 'annotation', 'TypeVar bound', 'type alias', and 'type parameter' as possible return values. Changed in version 3.13: Return values are members of the SymbolTableType enumeration. The exact values of the returned string may change in the future, and thus, it is recommended to use SymbolTabl...

info.excerpt: A namespace table for a block. The constructor is not public. get_type()¶ Return the type of the symbol table. Possible values are members of the SymbolTableType enumeration. Changed in version 3.12: ...

description.en: An enumeration indicating the type of a SymbolTable object. MODULE = "module"¶ Used for the symbol table of a module. FUNCTION = "function"¶ Used for the symbol table of a function. CLASS = "class"¶ Used for the symbol table of a class. The following members refer to different flavors of annotation scopes. ANNOTATION = "annotation"¶ Used for annotations if from __future__ import annotations is active. TYPE_ALIAS = "type alias"¶ Used for the symbol table of type constructions. TYPE_PARAMETERS ...

description.en: Return the toplevel SymbolTable for the Python source code. filename is the name of the file containing the code. compile_type is like the mode argument to compile().

info.excerpt: Return the toplevel SymbolTable for the Python source code. filename is the name of the file containing the code. compile_type is like the mode argument to compile().
